theme: quarantine and immunization on a contact network
title: The Frontier Ring: Quarantine on a Contact Chain
innovation_hook: Immunize the ring just ahead of the diffusion frontier to cut transmission paths before they are traversed, not the already-exposed core.
strategies: trivial: spend no budget at all (the evaluator's own do-nothing weak reference, ~0.1)

greedy (the trap): rank all nodes ONCE by static whole-graph degree and immunize the top-ranked nodes first, filling each round up to the per-round cap until the total budget is spent -- ignores which nodes are already infected/recovered and where the outbreak's frontier currently is, so it wastes budget on the origin cluster's own (already-infected) hub and on a densely-connected decoy cluster's globally-highest-degree hub that the outbreak reaches late or never, while the real chokepoints go unprotected

strong: simulate the deterministic outbreak forward internally and, each round, immunize the CURRENT frontier ring (susceptible nodes adjacent to an infectious node) ranked by remaining future-exposure potential, reallocating the scarce total budget to wherever the outbreak is about to go next instead of to a static global ranking

further headroom: predictive multi-round lookahead (immunize nodes that will become frontier 2-3 rounds ahead, not just the immediate ring), or a min-cut/flow formulation over the time-expanded graph to choose the cheapest set of edges-worth-of-nodes to sever across the whole horizon at once, rather than a per-round greedy ring
